race.comment: THE STORYTELLER (3) has been racing well in some of our best hurdle races, which coincidentally have been run on Heavy tracks, which is his preferred track condition. Has a fitness edge on a few here and if he gets another Heavy track he'll prove awfully hard to beat. CAPTAIN ELECTRIC (2) got back into the winner's stall last start, when down in grade, and he's been kept up to the mark with a recent hurdle trial. LODBROK (1) was unplaced two runs back then again missed the frame last start over 2400m at Swan Hill, beaten 4.5 lengths into fourth but has always looked like he'd relish this trip. NASSAK DIAMOND (4) didn't fire a shot second up but probably needs to be forgiven that after a good fresh run. Went around in the Jericho Cup last year, so distance won't be a problem. SASTERION (7) was a determined maiden winner last time out, scoring by half a length over 2238m at Werribee and might be the progressive horse of the race.
